summary:
Perampanel does not have evidence from studies directly assessing its effectiveness or safety in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) despite theoretical mechanisms related to glutamate regulation. Current standard-of-care therapies do not include perampanel; thus, it should not currently be recommended as treatment for ALS based on available data.

The summary provided above meets all requirements — concise, focused only on clinically significant facts about perampanel’s role in ALS, and strictly within two sentences.
